zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne於大約1200年出生在法國布格時，她的父親Etienne de Auxonne35歲，母親Beatrix de Châlon32歲。她和Ulrich II de Ferrette至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1277年3月31日在法國布格-福朗-可特艾豪-薩o瓦丹斯享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne于大约1200年出生在法国布格时，她的父亲Etienne de Auxonne35岁，母亲Beatrix de Châlon32岁。她和Ulrich II de Ferrette至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于1277年3月31日在法国布格-福朗-可特艾豪-薩o瓦丹斯享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourgogne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bourgoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphee, Alyre, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Celina, Emile, Gilberte, Jacques, Laurent, Lucienne, Olivier.

variant of Bourgouin (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a habitational name for someone from Burgundy (Old French Bourgogne), a region of eastern France having Dijon as its center. The area was invaded by the Burgundii, an ancient Germanic tribe from whom it takes its name, in about AD 480. The duchy of Burgundy, created in 877 by Charles II, King of the Western Franks, was extremely powerful in the later Middle Ages, especially under Philip the Bold (1342–1404; duke from 1363). Compare Bourgoyne and Bourguignon .

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne was born about 1200, in Burgundy, France, her father, Etienne de Auxonne, was 35 and her mother, Beatrix de Châlon, was 32. She had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Ulrich II de Ferrette. She died on 31 March 1277, in Vadans, Haute-Saône, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne est née environ 1200, à Bourgogne, France, son père, Etienne de Auxonne, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Châlon, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Ulrich II de Ferrette. Elle est décédée le 31 mars 1277, à Vadans, Haute-Saône, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne um 1200 in Burgund, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Etienne de Auxonne 35 und ihre Mutter Beatrix de Châlon 32. Sie hatte mit Ulrich II de Ferrette mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 31. März 1277 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Vadans, Haute-Saône, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne è nata il circa 1200 nel Bourgogne, Francia, sua padre, Etienne de Auxonne, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Beatrix de Châlon, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Ulrich II de Ferrette. Morì il 31 marzo 1277, nel Vadans, Haute-Saône, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 78 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne(이)가 대략 1200년 프랑스 부르고그니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Etienne de Auxonne(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix de Châlon(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Ulrich II de Ferrette(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1277년 3월 31일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 하테-사o니 바던스에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Ulrich II de Ferrette. Ela faleceu em 31 de março de 1277, em Vadans, Haute-Saône, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth de Chalons de Bourgogne родилась около 1200 г. в местности бургогн, Франция, её отцу по имени Etienne de Auxonne было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Châlon, 32. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Ulrich II de Ferrette. Она умерла 31 марта 1277 г. в местности ваданс, хауте-саoн, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 78 лет.
